assessments.name: Depression Screening (PHQ-9)

questions.text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Poor appetite or overeating
Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let your family down
Trouble concentrating on things
Moving or speaking so slowly that others have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or hurting yourself

assessments.interpretation: This is a quick screening tool. If scoring indicates moderate to severe depression, professional help is recommended.

assessments.name: Anxiety Assessment (GAD-7)

questions.text: Feeling nervous, anxious or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Trouble relaxing
Being so restless that it is hard to sit still
Becoming easily annoyed or irritable
Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

assessments.interpretation: Anxiety is normal, but persistent high levels warrant professional support.

assessments.name: Stress Evaluation

questions.text: How often do you feel overwhelmed by responsibilities?
How frequently does work/study interfere with your personal life?
How often do you experience physical symptoms (headaches, muscle tension)?
How much difficulty do you have managing multiple tasks?
How often do you feel emotionally exhausted?

assessments.interpretation: Regular stress management practices are recommended based on your score.

assessments.name: Sleep Quality Assessment

questions.text: How long does it take you to fall asleep?
How often do you wake during the night?
How refreshed do you feel after sleep?
Do you have nightmares or bad dreams?
Overall sleep quality

assessments.interpretation: Sleep quality significantly impacts mental health. Consider sleep hygiene improvements if needed.

assessments.name: Emotional Intelligence Assessment

questions.text: I can identify and name my emotions accurately
I can manage my emotions in challenging situations
I understand what motivates me
I can empathize with others' feelings
I manage relationships effectively

assessments.interpretation: Emotional intelligence can be developed through practice and self-awareness.

assessments.name: Relationship Satisfaction Scale

questions.text: My relationship is emotionally fulfilling
My partner and I communicate effectively
I feel supported and understood
We handle conflicts constructively
Overall, I am satisfied with this relationship

assessments.interpretation: Healthy relationships require ongoing communication and effort.
